title: ficus muelleri
intro: Ficus muelleri, also known as the forest ficus, is a species of fig tree native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its leaves and roots are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ficus muelleri include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Ficus muelleri include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from its leaves and roots, which are also used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
